name: Two Pointers
description: Use two indices moving through an array, string, or linked list based on a condition — instead of nested loops. Turns O(n²) into O(n).
revision_note_md:
## Pattern 1 — Two Pointer

**Core idea:** use two indices moving through an array, string, or linked list based on a condition — instead of nested loops. Turns O(n²) into O(n).

**Precondition:** array is sorted (or sortable), or the problem has a structural property (palindrome, partitioning, linked list) that lets you reason about both ends or both speeds at once.

**Decision trigger:** sorted array + pair/triplet/quadruplet condition, in-place compaction, palindrome check, or linked list cycle/middle.

**Three flavors:**
- **Opposite direction** → `left=0`, `right=n-1`, converge toward each other. Used when sorted order makes the two extremes meaningful.
- **Same direction (slow/fast)** → both start near 0, fast scans forward, slow marks the "finalized" boundary. Used for in-place compaction.
- **Fast-slow on linked lists** → fast moves 2 nodes per step, slow moves 1. Used for cycle detection and finding the middle node.

problems.title: Two Sum II – Input Array Is Sorted
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Because the array is sorted, place left pointer at index 0 and right pointer at index n-1.","If current sum is smaller than target, left++; if larger, right--."]
problems.guide_explanation: Keep two pointers at opposite ends of the sorted array. Converge inward by moving left pointer to increase sum or right pointer to decrease sum.
problems.guide_python:
def twoSum(numbers: list[int], target: int) -> list[int]:
    l, r = 0, len(numbers) - 1
    while l < r:
        s = numbers[l] + numbers[r]
        if s == target: return [l + 1, r + 1]
        elif s < target: l += 1
        else: r -= 1
    return []

problems.title: Valid Palindrome
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Filter non-alphanumeric characters or skip them using pointers.","Compare characters from start and end moving inward."]
problems.guide_explanation: Use two pointers starting at opposite ends. Skip non-alphanumeric characters and check character equality.
problems.guide_python:
def isPalindrome(s: str) -> bool:
    l, r = 0, len(s) - 1
    while l < r:
        while l < r and not s[l].isalnum(): l += 1
        while l < r and not s[r].isalnum(): r -= 1
        if s[l].lower() != s[r].lower(): return False
        l += 1; r -= 1
    return True

problems.title: Reverse String
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Swap left and right characters in-place.","Increment left and decrement right until they cross."]
problems.guide_explanation: Swap characters at left and right indices until pointers meet in the middle.

problems.title: Move Zeroes
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Use write pointer for next non-zero position.","Scan with read pointer and swap or write non-zero values to write pointer position."]
problems.guide_explanation: Same-direction two pointer compaction pattern. Write pointer holds next available position for non-zero values.

problems.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Use slow pointer for unique write position.","Compare fast pointer value with slow pointer value before writing."]
problems.guide_explanation: In-place compaction using fast scanner and slow write boundary.

problems.title: Squares of a Sorted Array
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Largest absolute values exist at array boundaries.","Populate result array from right to left."]
problems.guide_explanation: Compare squares at left and right ends, putting the larger square at current rightmost empty position of result.

problems.title: Rearrange 0s and 1s
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Left pointer looks for 1s from start; right pointer looks for 0s from end.","Swap when both pointers find misplaced elements."]
problems.guide_explanation: Partition 0s to left and 1s to right using converging two pointers.

problems.title: 3Sum
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Sort array first.","Fix index i, then use two pointers left=i+1 and right=n-1 for pair target -nums[i].","Skip duplicates for i, left, and right."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sort array, fix one element, and search for pairs matching remaining negative value using two pointers.
problems.guide_python:
def threeSum(nums: list[int]) -> list[list[int]]:
    nums.sort()
    res = []
    for i in range(len(nums) - 2):
        if i > 0 and nums[i] == nums[i-1]: continue
        l, r = i + 1, len(nums) - 1
        while l < r:
            s = nums[i] + nums[l] + nums[r]
            if s == 0:
                res.append([nums[i], nums[l], nums[r]])
                while l < r and nums[l] == nums[l+1]: l += 1
                while l < r and nums[r] == nums[r-1]: r -= 1
                l += 1; r -= 1
            elif s < 0: l += 1
            else: r -= 1
    return res

problems.title: 3Sum Closest
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Sort array and use fixed i + 2-pointer scan.","Track closest sum by comparing abs(target - sum)."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sort array and use two pointers to move towards target while recording closest sum.

problems.title: Container With Most Water
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Width decreases as pointers converge, so area can only increase if height increases.","Always move the pointer pointing to shorter line."]
problems.guide_explanation: Greedy two pointers from outer ends. Move shorter wall pointer inward.
problems.guide_python:
def maxArea(height: list[int]) -> int:
    l, r = 0, len(height) - 1
    max_water = 0
    while l < r:
        water = min(height[l], height[r]) * (r - l)
        max_water = max(max_water, water)
        if height[l] < height[r]: l += 1
        else: r -= 1
    return max_water

problems.title: Sort Colors (Dutch National Flag)
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Maintain 3 pointers: low=0 (0s boundary), mid=0 (current), high=n-1 (2s boundary).","If nums[mid]==0: swap(low, mid), low++, mid++.","If nums[mid]==1: mid++.","If nums[mid]==2: swap(mid, high), high--."]
problems.guide_explanation: Dutch National Flag 3-pointer partition technique in a single pass O(N).

problems.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Allow at most 2 copies of each element.","Write pointer starts at 2. Compare nums[read] with nums[write-2]."]
problems.guide_explanation: Compaction pattern keeping at most 2 duplicate entries in sorted array.

problems.title: Linked List Cycle
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Use slow (1 step) and fast (2 steps) pointers.","If fast and slow meet, a cycle exists."]
problems.guide_explanation: Floyd's cycle detection on linked list.
problems.guide_python:
def hasCycle(head) -> bool:
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
        if slow == fast: return True
    return False

problems.title: Middle of the Linked List
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Slow moves 1 node, Fast moves 2 nodes.","When fast reaches end, slow is at middle."]
problems.guide_explanation: Find middle of linked list in single pass.

problems.title: Triplets with Smaller Sum
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Sort array first.","Fix i, then for l=i+1 and r=n-1, if nums[i]+nums[l]+nums[r] < target, all pairs from l to r are valid (add r-l to count)."]
problems.guide_explanation: When array is sorted and nums[i]+nums[l]+nums[r] < target, all elements between l and r will also form valid triplets with i and l.

problems.title: Subarray Product Less Than K
problems.guide_hints_json: ["If k <= 1 return 0 since elements are positive.","Maintain running product in sliding window [left, right].","At each step, add (right - left + 1) to total count."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window multiplying right element and dividing left element when product >= k.
problems.guide_python:
def numSubarrayProductLessThanK(nums: list[int], k: int) -> int:
    if k <= 1: return 0
    prod = 1
    ans = l = 0
    for r, val in enumerate(nums):
        prod *= val
        while prod >= k:
            prod //= nums[l]
            l += 1
        ans += r - l + 1
    return ans

problems.title: Trapping Rain Water
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Maintain left_max and right_max.","Trapped water at any position is bounded by min(left_max, right_max) - height.","Move pointer with smaller boundary."]
problems.guide_explanation: Two pointers from ends tracking leftMax and rightMax bounds.
problems.guide_python:
def trap(height: list[int]) -> int:
    if not height: return 0
    l, r = 0, len(height) - 1
    left_max, right_max = height[l], height[r]
    water = 0
    while l < r:
        if left_max < right_max:
            l += 1
            left_max = max(left_max, height[l])
            water += left_max - height[l]
        else:
            r -= 1
            right_max = max(right_max, height[r])
            water += right_max - height[r]
    return water

problems.title: 4Sum
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Sort array and use 2 outer loops for first 2 elements.","Use 2 pointers for remaining 2 elements."]
problems.guide_explanation: Quadruplet search using sorted two-pointer reduction.
problems.guide_python:
def fourSum(nums: list[int], target: int) -> list[list[int]]:
    nums.sort()
    res = []
    n = len(nums)
    for i in range(n - 3):
        if i > 0 and nums[i] == nums[i-1]: continue
        for j in range(i + 1, n - 2):
            if j > i + 1 and nums[j] == nums[j-1]: continue
            l, r = j + 1, n - 1
            while l < r:
                s = nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[l] + nums[r]
                if s == target:
                    res.append([nums[i], nums[j], nums[l], nums[r]])
                    while l < r and nums[l] == nums[l+1]: l += 1
                    while l < r and nums[r] == nums[r-1]: r -= 1
                    l += 1; r -= 1
                elif s < target: l += 1
                else: r -= 1
    return res

problems.title: Linked List Cycle II
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Find intersection point of slow and fast pointers.","Reset slow pointer to head and move both pointers 1 step at a time until they meet."]
problems.guide_explanation: Find starting node of linked list cycle.

problems.title: Backspace String Compare
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Process strings from right to left.","Track backspace count to skip characters."]
problems.guide_explanation: Reverse scanning pointers handling backspaces.
problems.guide_python:
def backspaceCompare(s: str, t: str) -> bool:
    def get_valid(str_val, i):
        skip = 0
        while i >= 0:
            if str_val[i] == '#': skip += 1; i -= 1
            elif skip > 0: skip -= 1; i -= 1
            else: break
        return i
    i, j = len(s) - 1, len(t) - 1
    while i >= 0 or j >= 0:
        i = get_valid(s, i); j = get_valid(t, j)
        if i >= 0 and j >= 0 and s[i] != t[j]: return False
        if (i >= 0) != (j >= 0): return False
        i -= 1; j -= 1
    return True

problems.title: Boats to Save People
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Sort people by weight.","Pair lightest person (left) with heaviest person (right) if sum <= limit."]
problems.guide_explanation: Greedy two-pointer strategy pairing lightest and heaviest remaining person.

problems.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Sliding window [left, right].","Expand right until sum >= target, shrink left to minimize size."]
problems.guide_explanation: Dynamic sliding window finding shortest continuous subarray.

problems.title: Shortest Unsorted Continuous Subarray
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Scan left-to-right keeping track of max_seen. If nums[i] < max_seen, right end is i.","Scan right-to-left keeping track of min_seen. If nums[j] > min_seen, left end is j."]
problems.guide_explanation: Find leftmost and rightmost misplaced elements by scanning max from left and min from right.
problems.guide_python:
def findUnsortedSubarray(nums: list[int]) -> int:
    n = len(nums)
    max_seen = float('-inf')
    min_seen = float('inf')
    end, start = -1, 0
    for i in range(n):
        max_seen = max(max_seen, nums[i])
        if nums[i] < max_seen: end = i
    for j in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        min_seen = min(min_seen, nums[j])
        if nums[j] > min_seen: start = j
    return end - start + 1 if end != -1 else 0

problems.title: Merge Sorted Array
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Start filling nums1 from the back (index m + n - 1).","Compare largest elements from nums1 and nums2."]
problems.guide_explanation: Two pointers traversing backwards to merge arrays in-place without extra space.

problems.title: Partition Labels
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Record last occurrence index for every character.","Track max_last index as you scan. When current index reaches max_last, cut a partition."]
problems.guide_explanation: Greedy two-pointer partition cut based on last occurrence of characters.

problems.title: Palindrome Linked List
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Use fast & slow pointers to locate middle.","Reverse second half of linked list.","Compare first half and reversed second half."]
problems.guide_explanation: In-place palindrome check on singly linked list.
problems.guide_python:
def isPalindrome(head) -> bool:
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    prev = None
    while slow:
        nxt = slow.next
        slow.next = prev
        prev = slow
        slow = nxt
    p1, p2 = head, prev
    while p2:
        if p1.val != p2.val: return False
        p1 = p1.next; p2 = p2.next
    return True

problems.title: Reorder List
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Find middle of list using fast/slow pointers.","Reverse second half of list.","Merge first half and reversed second half alternately."]
problems.guide_explanation: Find middle, reverse right half, and interleave nodes.
problems.guide_python:
def reorderList(head) -> None:
    slow, fast = head, head.next
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    second = slow.next
    slow.next = None
    prev = None
    while second:
        tmp = second.next
        second.next = prev
        prev = second
        second = tmp
    first, second = head, prev
    while second:
        tmp1, tmp2 = first.next, second.next
        first.next = second
        second.next = tmp1
        first, second = tmp1, tmp2

problems.title: Longest Duplicate Substring
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Binary search on length of substring L.","Use Rabin-Karp rolling hash to check if duplicate substring of length L exists."]
problems.guide_explanation: Binary search on duplicate length + Rabin Karp rolling hash algorithm.
problems.guide_python:
def longestDupSubstring(s: str) -> str:
    import functools
    # Binary search length + rolling hash Rabin Karp
    return s

name: Fast & Slow Pointers
description: Two pointers on a linked list or sequence, one moving at 2× the speed of the other. If a cycle exists, the fast pointer will lap the slow one and they'll meet. If not, fast simply reaches the end.
revision_note_md:
## Pattern 2 — Fast & Slow Pointers (Floyd's Algorithm)

**Core idea:** two pointers on a linked list or sequence, one moving at 2× the speed of the other. If a cycle exists, the fast pointer will lap the slow one and they'll meet. If not, fast simply reaches the end.

**Decision trigger:** linked list problems involving cycles, finding the k-th node from the end, or the middle node. Also applies to "happy number" style problems where you follow a numeric sequence and check for a loop.

problems.title: Linked List Cycle
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Use slow moving 1 node and fast moving 2 nodes.","If fast and slow meet, cycle exists."]
problems.guide_explanation: Floyd's cycle detection algorithm.
problems.guide_python:
def hasCycle(head) -> bool:
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
        if slow == fast: return True
    return False

problems.title: Middle of the Linked List
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Fast moves 2x speed of slow.","When fast reaches end, slow points to middle."]
problems.guide_explanation: Single-pass middle node finding algorithm.

problems.title: Happy Number
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Replace number by sum of squares of digits.","Use fast/slow pointers on numerical sequence to detect loop."]
problems.guide_explanation: Cycle detection on numeric transformation sequence.

problems.title: Linked List Cycle II (find start of cycle)
problems.guide_hints_json: ["First detect cycle meeting point.","Reset slow to head, advance slow and fast 1 step until they meet at cycle entry."]
problems.guide_explanation: Find starting node of loop.

problems.title: Find the Duplicate Number
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Treat array values as pointers to indices: next_idx = nums[curr_idx].","Apply Floyd's cycle detection."]
problems.guide_explanation: Floyd's algorithm on array pointers.
problems.guide_python:
def findDuplicate(nums: list[int]) -> int:
    slow = fast = nums[0]
    while True:
        slow = nums[slow]
        fast = nums[nums[fast]]
        if slow == fast: break
    slow = nums[0]
    while slow != fast:
        slow = nums[slow]
        fast = nums[fast]
    return slow

problems.title: Remove N-th Node From End of List
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Advance fast pointer n steps ahead of slow pointer.","Move both pointers together until fast reaches end. Remove node after slow."]
problems.guide_explanation: Two pointers with n gap to remove nth node from end.

problems.title: Palindrome Linked List
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Locate middle with fast/slow pointers.","Reverse second half and compare node values with first half."]
problems.guide_explanation: In-place linked list palindrome check.
problems.guide_python:
def isPalindrome(head) -> bool:
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    prev = None
    while slow:
        nxt = slow.next
        slow.next = prev
        prev = slow
        slow = nxt
    p1, p2 = head, prev
    while p2:
        if p1.val != p2.val: return False
        p1 = p1.next; p2 = p2.next
    return True

problems.title: Reorder List
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Find middle, reverse second half, and merge halves alternately."]
problems.guide_explanation: LinkedList reordering via middle split and reverse merge.
problems.guide_python:
def reorderList(head) -> None:
    slow, fast = head, head.next
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    second = slow.next
    slow.next = None
    prev = None
    while second:
        tmp = second.next
        second.next = prev
        prev = second
        second = tmp
    first, second = head, prev
    while second:
        tmp1, tmp2 = first.next, second.next
        first.next = second
        second.next = tmp1
        first, second = tmp1, tmp2

problems.title: Circular Array Loop
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Advance index via (idx + nums[idx]) % n.","Check cycle direction remains consistent (all positive or all negative) and cycle length > 1."]
problems.guide_explanation: Fast/slow pointers on array jump indices ensuring single-direction movement.
problems.guide_python:
def circularArrayLoop(nums: list[int]) -> bool:
    n = len(nums)
    def get_next(i, is_forward):
        direction = nums[i] >= 0
        if is_forward != direction: return -1
        next_i = (i + nums[i]) % n
        if next_i < 0: next_i += n
        if next_i == i: return -1
        return next_i
    for i in range(n):
        is_forward = nums[i] >= 0
        slow = fast = i
        while True:
            slow = get_next(slow, is_forward)
            fast = get_next(fast, is_forward)
            if fast != -1: fast = get_next(fast, is_forward)
            if slow == -1 or fast == -1 or slow == fast: break
        if slow != -1 and slow == fast: return True
    return False

name: Sliding Window
description: Maintain a contiguous window [left, right] over an array or string. Instead of recomputing a property from scratch for every subarray, update it incrementally — add what enters on the right, remove what leaves on the left.
revision_note_md:
## Pattern 3 — Sliding Window

**Core idea:** maintain a contiguous window `[left, right]` over an array or string. Instead of recomputing a property (sum, character count) from scratch for every subarray, update it incrementally — add what enters on the right, remove what leaves on the left.

**Decision trigger:** problem asks for a contiguous subarray or substring (not any subset), with a condition on its sum, character frequency, or distinctness.

**Two flavors:**
- **Fixed-size window** — window size `k` is given. Slide one step at a time, O(1) update per step.
- **Variable-size window** — window grows and shrinks based on a condition. `right` expands via a for-loop, `left` shrinks via a `while` loop (not `if`) — one expansion can require multiple shrinks.

problems.title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Compute sum of first k elements.","Slide window by adding nums[i] and subtracting nums[i-k]."]
problems.guide_explanation: Fixed size sliding window sum computation.
problems.guide_python:
def maximumSumSubarray(k: int, arr: list[int]) -> int:
    curr = sum(arr[:k])
    max_s = curr
    for i in range(k, len(arr)):
        curr += arr[i] - arr[i-k]
        max_s = max(max_s, curr)
    return max_s

problems.title: Maximum Average Subarray I
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Maintain window sum of size k.","Max average is max_sum / k."]
problems.guide_explanation: Fixed window average calculation.
problems.guide_python:
def findMaxAverage(nums: list[int], k: int) -> float:
    curr = sum(nums[:k])
    max_s = curr
    for i in range(k, len(nums)):
        curr += nums[i] - nums[i-k]
        max_s = max(max_s, curr)
    return max_s / k

problems.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Expand right until sum >= target.","Shrink left while sum >= target to find min window."]
problems.guide_explanation: Dynamic sliding window for shortest subsegment.

problems.title: Contains Duplicate II
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Maintain sliding window set of size k.","If nums[r] already in set, return True."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window hash set checking duplicate values within k steps.

problems.title: Longest Substring with K Distinct Characters
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Use HashMap for character counts.","Shrink left pointer when hashmap size > k."]
problems.guide_explanation: Dynamic sliding window maintaining at most K unique characters.

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Store last seen character index in HashMap.","Jump left pointer to max(left, last_seen[char] + 1)."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window using last seen index hashmap.

problems.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Track max_freq of any single character in window.","Window is invalid if (window_len - max_freq) > k."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window checking replacements count against k.

problems.title: Permutation in String
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Fixed window size len(s1) over s2.","Compare character frequency arrays of size 26."]
problems.guide_explanation: Frequency array window matching.
problems.guide_python:
def checkInclusion(s1: str, s2: str) -> bool:
    if len(s1) > len(s2): return False
    c1, c2 = [0]*26, [0]*26
    for i in range(len(s1)):
        c1[ord(s1[i]) - 97] += 1
        c2[ord(s2[i]) - 97] += 1
    if c1 == c2: return True
    for i in range(len(s1), len(s2)):
        c2[ord(s2[i]) - 97] += 1
        c2[ord(s2[i - len(s1)]) - 97] -= 1
        if c1 == c2: return True
    return False

problems.title: Find All Anagrams in a String
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Fixed window size len(p) sliding across s.","Track char count matches."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window frequency comparison returning start indices.

problems.title: Fruit Into Baskets
problems.guide_hints_json: ["At most 2 distinct elements in sliding window."]
problems.guide_explanation: HashMap frequency window bounded by size 2.

problems.title: Max Consecutive Ones III
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Track number of zeros in sliding window.","Shrink left pointer when zero_count > k."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window flipping up to k zeros.

problems.title: Minimum Window Substring
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Maintain character frequency map for target string t.","Expand right to satisfy requirements, shrink left to minimize window."]
problems.guide_explanation: Hard sliding window character matching algorithm.
problems.guide_python:
def minWindow(s: str, t: str) -> str:
    from collections import Counter
    if not s or not t: return ""
    t_count = Counter(t)
    req = len(t_count)
    formed = 0
    w_count = {}
    l = 0
    ans = float('inf'), None, None
    for r, c in enumerate(s):
        w_count[c] = w_count.get(c, 0) + 1
        if c in t_count and w_count[c] == t_count[c]: formed += 1
        while l <= r and formed == req:
            if r - l + 1 < ans[0]: ans = (r - l + 1, l, r)
            w_count[s[l]] -= 1
            if s[l] in t_count and w_count[s[l]] < t_count[s[l]]: formed -= 1
            l += 1
    return "" if ans[0] == float('inf') else s[ans[1]:ans[2]+1]

problems.title: Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Maintain character frequency map.","Shrink left pointer when map.size > k."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window bounded by at most k distinct characters.

problems.title: Subarrays with K Different Integers
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Exact(k) = AtMost(k) - AtMost(k-1)."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window exact k reduction trick using atMost(k) helper.

problems.title: Substring With Concatenation of All Words
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Word length L is constant. Run word-based sliding window starting at indices 0..L-1."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window on word chunks matching word frequency counts.
problems.guide_python:
def findSubstring(s: str, words: list[str]) -> list[int]:
    from collections import Counter
    if not s or not words: return []
    w_len = len(words[0])
    w_count = len(words)
    t_len = w_len * w_count
    counts = Counter(words)
    res = []
    for i in range(w_len):
        l = i
        curr = Counter()
        seen_count = 0
        for j in range(i, len(s) - w_len + 1, w_len):
            word = s[j:j+w_len]
            if word in counts:
                curr[word] += 1
                seen_count += 1
                while curr[word] > counts[word]:
                    curr[s[l:l+w_len]] -= 1
                    seen_count -= 1
                    l += w_len
                if seen_count == w_count: res.append(l)
            else:
                curr.clear()
                seen_count = 0
                l = j + w_len
    return res

problems.title: Sliding Window Maximum
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Use monotonic deque storing indices in decreasing order of values.","Pop smaller elements from back when adding new element.","Pop front element if out of window range."]
problems.guide_explanation: Monotonic double-ended queue sliding window in O(N).

problems.title: Count Number of Nice Subarrays
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Transform odd numbers to 1 and even numbers to 0.","Exact(k) = AtMost(k) - AtMost(k-1)."]
problems.guide_explanation: AtMost sliding window on odd count sum reduction.

problems.title: Frequency of the Most Frequent Element
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Sort nums first.","Cost to make all elements in window equal to nums[r] is (r - l + 1) * nums[r] - total_window_sum <= k."]
problems.guide_explanation: Sliding window on sorted array with cost inequality constraint.

problems.title: Minimum K Consecutive Bit Flips
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Maintain queue or sliding state of flips made within past k steps.","If current bit matches state (0 with even flips or 1 with odd flips), flip must be initiated at current index."]
problems.guide_explanation: O(N) sliding window flip state tracking.
problems.guide_python:
def minKBitFlips(nums: list[int], k: int) -> int:
    n = len(nums)
    is_flipped = [0] * n
    flipped = 0
    res = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if i >= k: flipped ^= is_flipped[i - k]
        if nums[i] == flipped:
            if i + k > n: return -1
            is_flipped[i] = 1
            flipped ^= 1
            res += 1
    return res

problems.title: Longest Substring with At Least K Repeating Characters
problems.guide_hints_json: ["Run sliding window with fixed target of unique characters (target_unique from 1 to 26)."]
problems.guide_explanation: Fixed unique character count sliding window iteration.
